title: What Scripture Actually Says About Self-Discipline
caption:
Most men treat self-discipline like a scoreboard. Hit the target, earn God's favor. Miss it, subtract.

That's not what Scripture teaches.

Self-control in Galatians 5 is fruit of the Spirit. Not a badge. Not a performance review. It grows when a man surrenders, not when he performs. The man who gets that order right isn't grinding for God's approval — he already has it. He's just growing into what God already says he is.

Hard on yourself, yes. Harsh on yourself, no. The difference is the difference between formation and exhaustion.

"It is for freedom that Christ has set us free." — Galatians 5:1

Save this for the man who thinks his discipline is what earns him something.

#faith #selfdiscipline #christianmen #galatians5 #godlymen #manofgod #scripture #formation #christianity
theme: He's hard on himself, gentle with others.
